macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Closes Lower for Third Straight Day, Below 150-Day Moving Average
items.summary: The S&P 500 finished at its lowest close since November 21, dropping below its 150-day moving average for the first time since May amid ongoing market weakness. Despite intraday recoveries, the index has declined for three consecutive days.

items.title: US Inflation Rises 0.3% in February, Matching Estimates at 2.4% Yearly
items.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a 0.3% monthly increase in inflation for last month, with annual inflation at 2.4%; core inflation rose 0.2% monthly and 2.5% yearly, remaining above the Fed's 2% target.

items.title: Fed Unlikely to Cut Rates Next Week Amid Job Losses and Persistent Inflation
items.summary: The economy has lost jobs in three of the last five months, but the Fed is not expected to cut rates at its upcoming meeting due to inflation above target; analysts doubt cuts in the next six months as recession risks rise.

items.title: S&P 500 Futures Prediction Markets Favor Levels Above 6575 on March 13
items.summary: Robinhood prediction markets show strong bets (95¢ per contract) for Mar-26 S&P 500 futures settling above 6575, with 84¢ for above 6600 and 67¢ for above 6650, indicating market expectations for stability or gains.

items.title: Nasdaq-100 Futures Prediction Markets Bet on Levels Above 24200
items.summary: Prediction markets on Robinhood price contracts at 81¢ for Mar-26 Nasdaq-100 futures above 24200, but only 16¢ for above 24900, reflecting cautious optimism amid recent market declines.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Pierces Resistance Above $74,500 Amid Technical Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin has broken above key resistance levels, triggering a short squeeze for BTC and altcoins. However, daily RSI is approaching overbought territory, with analysts expecting a potential reversal as traditional finance shows bearish signals from Middle East tensions.

items.title: BlackRock Launches Ethereum Staking ETF
items.summary: BlackRock has launched an Ethereum staking ETF, potentially enabling a rotation into ETH as investors seek assets to outpace inflation amid global economic uncertainty.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Iran Crisis, Impacting Crypto Markets
items.summary: WTI crude oil has risen approximately 9% in a single day to $96, while Brent crude exceeded $100 per barrel following escalating Middle East tensions and Iran's new leadership taking a hardline stance on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Eric Trump's Bitcoin Mining Company ABTC Down Nearly 90%
items.summary: American Bitcoin Corp (ABTC), co-founded by Eric Trump in early 2025 and listed on NASDAQ in September 2025 at a $5 billion valuation, has seen retail investors suffer losses of nearly 90% due to aggressive share dilution for Bitcoin purchases.

items.title: Crypto Market Faces Headwinds from Traditional Finance Downturn
items.summary: Analysts warn that cryptocurrency markets remain unaware of an impending traditional finance crash driven by Middle East geopolitical risks and Japanese Yen strength, with expectations for broader market correction.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Databricks Acquires Quotient AI to Boost AI Agent Evaluation
items.summary: Databricks acquired Quotient AI, a startup specializing in evaluation and reinforcement learning for AI agents, to enhance deployment, monitoring, and optimization in production. The technology will integrate with products like Genie and Agent Bricks, founded by ex-GitHub Copilot engineers.[1]

items.title: Google Uses Gemini AI and News Reports to Predict Flash Floods
items.summary: Google researchers leveraged its Gemini large language model to analyze 5 million news articles, creating a geo-tagged dataset of 2.6 million floods called 'Groundsource' for improved flash flood prediction. The approach could extend to forecasting heat waves and mudslides.[3]

items.title: NIH-Funded Merlin AI Model Advances CT Scan Analysis
items.summary: The Merlin foundation model, trained on 15,000+ abdominal CT scans and reports, predicts diseases with 81% accuracy and future risks like diabetes up to five years ahead at 75% accuracy, outperforming specialized AI systems. It detects subtle biomarkers in routine medical scans.[4]

items.title: Konica Minolta and AIST Develop AI for Optimizing Plastic Molding
items.summary: Konica Minolta and Japan's AIST created multimodal AI models to predict optimal mixing and molding conditions for composite plastics using minimal data, ensuring consistent quality via materials and process informatics. The tech supports green transformation efforts.[5]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Confirms Marathon Release in March 2026 at $40
items.summary: Bungie and PlayStation announced that the extraction shooter Marathon will launch in March 2026 priced at $40, featuring improvements like proximity chat and solo modes following alpha feedback and a delay from September 2025.[1]

items.title: Nintendo Shares Surge $17.9 Billion on Pokemon Pokopia Success
items.summary: Nintendo's stock jumped $17.9 billion, its biggest weekly gain in a decade, driven by the surprise hit Pokemon Pokopia, a Switch 2 exclusive that sold 2.2 million copies in four days and received rave reviews for its cozy life sim gameplay.[2]

items.title: Blizzard Revives Classic Esports at BlizzCon 2026 with StarCraft and Warcraft III
items.summary: Blizzard announced the inaugural Blizzard Classic Cup at BlizzCon 2026 on September 12-13, featuring competitions in StarCraft: Remastered, StarCraft II, Warcraft III: Reforged, and Heroes of the Storm, captained by esports legends Tasteless and Artosis.[4]

items.title: Xsolla Report Highlights AI Gains and Cross-Platform Opportunities in Gaming
items.summary: Xsolla's new report reveals AI cutting development times by 20% and support costs by 40%, 43% of US gamers playing across platforms, and growth in live service models like PUBG and Roblox.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Major Tech Companies Pledge to Supply Own Power for Data Centers
items.summary: Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, and xAI signed a federal pledge to provide their own power for AI data centers to limit impact on consumer electricity costs. This addresses growing energy demands from tech infrastructure.

items.title: Big Tech Pours Billions into Nuclear Energy for Data Centers
items.summary: Major technology companies are investing billions in nuclear energy, including reactor restarts and small modular reactor (SMR) development, to power data centers amid surging electricity needs.

items.title: Datagrid Gains Consent for Massive 280MW Data Center in New Zealand
items.summary: Datagrid received full resource consent for a 78,000sqm data center in Makarewa, set to be New Zealand's second-largest electricity user at 280MW, promising jobs and fast internet but raising environmental concerns.

items.title: Software Stocks Face Pressure from AI Disruption Fears and Market Unwinds
items.summary: Markets showed concerns over software sector growth due to AI disruption, with short positioning unwinds and compressed multiples affecting tech valuations and IPO backlogs.
